topic.name: Legislative Designations and Authorizations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various acts and resolutions related to the designation of facilities, naming of geographic features, and authorizations for funding and grants within government and veteran affairs.

Document 1: Renames the Quinebaug and Shetucket Rivers Valley National Heritage Corridor in Connecticut and Massachusetts as the Last Green Valley National Heritage Corridor.
Document 2: Authorizes the Attorney General to make grants to states... for the renovation, modernization, and construction of law enforcement facilities.
Document 4: Designates the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) medical center in Spokane, Washington, as the 'Mann-Grandstaff Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center.'
Document 6: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from declaring as excess to the needs of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA)... federal land and improvements at the St. Albans campus, New York.
Document 8: Directs the United States Board on Geographic Names to designate... a specified unnamed peak in the Alaska Range near Denali National Park and Preserve in Alaska as 'Mount Stevens.'
Document 9: Designates the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) community based outpatient clinic in Mansfield, Ohio, as the 'David F. Winder Department of Veterans Affairs Community Based Outpatient Clinic.'
Document 10: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to create a new term of service for the office of Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI).

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments and provisions related to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax relief and incentives for different sectors and situations.

Document 1: Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 allowing a tax credit for employer-provided child care.
Document 2: Student Loan Tax Relief Act - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income income imputed from a discharge of student loan indebtedness.
Document 3: Civil Justice Tax Fairness Act of 2015 allows an exclusion from gross income for amounts received on account of a claim of unlawful discrimination.
Document 4: Wind Incentives for a New Decade Energy Act of 2009 extends the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable wind resources.
Document 5: Home Office Deduction Simplification Act allows a standard tax deduction for business use of a residence.
Document 6: Working American Competitiveness Act allows taxpayers to exclude earned income from foreign sources.
Document 7: Preserving Investment in Needy Neighborhoods Act excludes contributions to the capital of a partnership.
Document 8: Extends the period for carryover of excess contributions for charitable deductions from 5 to 20 years.
Document 9: Free Internet Filing Act enables direct filing of income tax returns through the IRS website.
Document 10: Restaurant and Retail Jobs and Growth Act makes permanent the 15-year recovery period for qualified leasehold improvement property.

topic.name: Educational Support and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at enhancing educational access, safety, and support for students, particularly those from vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Amends the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act's program of grants to states for the education of homeless youths
ensure that homeless youth do not lose credits earned in other schools
Document 2: Requires states and local educational agencies to obtain an FBI background check on individuals prior to offering them employment as school employees
Prohibits states or LEAs from hiring individuals as school employees if they have been convicted of a crime of violence or other felony
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award grants to states for programmatic initiatives aimed at improving academic achievement
Requires subgrant applicants to demonstrate that their schools have a history of significant truancy
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to implement a pilot program providing nutritious food to at-risk school children
Includes elementary and secondary schools, school food authorities, and emergency feeding agencies as eligible nonprofit recipients
Document 5: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to encourage the establishment of Teachers Institutes
Defines a Teachers Institute as a partnership between one or more IHEs and LEAs serving low-income populations
Document 6: Amends the Safe and Drug-Free Schools and Communities Act to require states to collect and report information on bullying and harassment
Requires LEAs to establish grievance procedures for students and parents to register complaints regarding such conduct
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to support school reform efforts
Requires each grant to be used to address areas of school reform, including improving the effectiveness of teachers
Document 8: Allows local educational agencies to use title II subgrants to train school personnel in restorative justice and conflict resolution
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive matching grants for the development of afterschool interdisciplinary education programs
Gives grant priority to programs targeting impoverished, struggling, or rural students
Document 10: Establishes a program awarding competitive grants to institutions of higher education for cybersecurity training
Urges the House of Representatives to designate a committee for cybersecurity oversight

topic.name: Medicaid and Medicare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments and acts related to Medicaid and Medicare programs in the United States.

1: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act...
Requires a state to have in operation...
2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to limit increases in the monthly Medicare premium...
3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to...
Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to authorize the public release of physician performance measurements.
6: Amends titles XVI (Supplemental Security Income) (SSI) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act...
7: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require state Medicaid plan coverage...
8: Amends part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to...
9: This bill amends part A (General Provisions) of title XI of the Social Security Act to authorize the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to waive applicable general and Medicaid requirements...
10: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to extend through April 1, 2009, the approval of specified waivers under title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act...

topic.name: Suspension of Tariffs
topic.description: This cluster discusses amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States concerning the temporary suspension of duties on various imported goods.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain woven mesh fabric.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain plastic fittings composed of perfluoroalkoxy (PFA) resin.
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain untwisted filament yarns.
Document 4: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain synthetic filament yarns.
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on aqueous dispersions of poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) poly(styrenesulfonate).
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on aqueous catalytic preparations based on iron (III) toluenesulfonate.
Document 7: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on 3 Chloro 4 Methyl Aniline.
Document 8: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on certain sector mold press machines.
Document 9: suspend temporarily the duty on reusable surgical drapes of textile materials.

topic.name: Veterans' Health and Support Programs
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at improving health care, housing, and employment support for veterans.

Document 1: Authorize the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to construct a major medical facility project
to meet the health care needs of veterans
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to establish a pilot program
to rehabilitate and modify the primary residence of disabled or low-income veterans
Document 3: Reauthorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs homeless veterans reintegration programs
to expedite the reintegration of homeless veterans into the labor force
Document 4: Authorizes the city of Aurora, Colorado, to convey nonfederal lands for construction of a veterans' medical facility
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Labor to pay to each covered veteran an assistance allowance
for each month such veteran is enrolled in an employment and training program
Document 6: Reduces the period during which a disabled veterans' outreach program specialist must complete the training program
Document 7: Prohibits the appropriation of funds by Secretary of Veterans Affairs for any VA alternative energy generation project
Document 8: Directs the VA to transfer all unobligated funds for an alternative energy generation project to veterans' medical care
Document 9: Requires grants to be used to rehabilitate and modify the primary residence of disabled or low-income veterans
Document 10: Directs the Department of Defense and the Department of Veterans Affairs to jointly establish a uniform formulary for critical drugs

topic.name: Foster Care and Child Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving foster care systems, child support enforcement, and related child welfare policies.

1: Modernizing the Interstate Placement of Children in Foster Care Act
7: eliminate the requirement that... a child would have been eligible for aid
9: increase the federal medical assistance percentage (FMAP) for foster care and adoption assistance
10: eligibility of a child for a kinship guardian assistance payment shall not be affected

topic.name: Consumer Credit Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumers in their use of credit and financial services.

Document 1: prohibit a creditor from imposing upon an open-end consumer credit account any fee related to failure of the consumer to use the credit card
requires any annual fee that a card issuer may impose... to be reasonable.
Document 2: increase limitations on the maximum original principal obligation of mortgages
will have a significant impact on the cost or availability of mortgages for such homes.
Document 3: require... each consumer reporting agency to make credit scores available to consumers once each year without charge
creditors... to disclose consumer credit scores that were used in making any determination.
Document 4: require that each covered mortgage servicer provide periodic notice to each mortgagor
notify the mortgagor of possible eligibility for federal or federally related homeowner assistance.
Document 5: set forth restrictions upon the termination of a consumer credit card account due to inactivity
Prohibits a consumer credit card account from being terminated for inactivity unless the cardholder is notified.
Document 6: prohibit an insured depository institution from charging a fee to any consumer for receiving or using a debit card
Subjects violators... to civil liability.
Document 7: prohibit a creditor from using any adverse information concerning any consumer
Requires such limitation on the use of adverse information to be clearly and conspicuously described to the consumer.
Document 8: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act with respect to mandatory fee disclosures
Limits such requirement to appearance of a fee disclosure on the ATM screen.
Document 9: repeals the requirement that such a fee disclosure appear in a prominent and conspicuous location
Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to make the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau... a 'covered agency'.
Document 10: specify an extenuating circumstance for purposes of determining if a borrower has an adverse credit history
an extenuating circumstance exists if... the borrower is 90 or more days delinquent on mortgage loan payments.

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to land management, conservation, and resource protection involving federal, state, and tribal authorities.

Document 1: amends the Tribal Forest Protection Act
establish a timeframe for tribal requests to protect Indian forest land
Document 2: makes supplemental appropriations to the Department of Agriculture
for cooperative land forest health management
Document 3: allows state or tribe to assume management functions
regard to wild free-roaming horses and burros
Document 5: Administrator may convey to the city any reversionary interest
prohibits anything in this Act from affecting environmental law
Document 6: permits Clark County to convey land to the Nevada Division of State Lands
provides for the reversion of such land
Document 9: directs the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal lands
managed in accordance with the terms of the Recreation and Public Purposes Act
Document 10: authorizes the Boy Scouts to convey public land for operating a camp
in exchange for conveyance by Brian Head Resort of certain of its private land

topic.name: Political Campaign Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating campaign contributions, political communication, and candidate transparency in federal elections.

1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission (FEC)
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC
3: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to revise the do-not-call registry provisions of the Telemarketing Sales Rule to prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls

Defines a 'politically-oriented recorded message telephone call' as an outbound telephone call that plays a recorded message the purpose of which is to promote, advertise, campaign, or solicit donations
5: This bill amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require any candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
A tax return furnished to the FEC by a candidate or by Treasury shall be treated in the same manner as a report filed by the candidate

topic.name: Immigration Enforcement Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating immigration status and enforcing laws related to undocumented immigrants in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits a person from receiving a federal public benefit unless such person is in 'satisfactory immigration status'
Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements (SAVE) program.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Attorney General to take into custody an alien who is unlawfully in the United States
Directs the officer to verify the individual's immigration status.
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of Labor from implementing rules related to H-2B program
Directs the Secretary to use specific data for computing prevailing occupational wage levels.
Document 4: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996
Makes a sanctuary city ineligible to receive federal funds.
Document 5: Allows employers to use the employment eligibility confirmation system to verify immigration status
Verifies identity and employment eligibility from the time of application.
Document 6: Directs DHS to establish a law enforcement notification system for every state
Secure information sharing system for notifying about aliens' custody.
Document 7: Prohibits sanctuary jurisdictions from receiving certain federal grants
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to increase prison terms for illegal reentry.
Document 8: Directs DHS to adjust to permanent resident status for qualifying Liberian nationals
Adjustment applications must be filed within one year.
Document 9: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to permit lawful aliens on student visas to attend U.S. public secondary schools longer than one year
Requires reimbursement for educational costs.
Document 10: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to permit joint petitions for H-2A visa workers
Simplifies the process for employers to rehire H-2A workers.

topic.name: Military Policy and Security
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation related to military personnel management, security, and international stability efforts involving the United States Armed Forces.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Defense to transfer members of the Armed Forces to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs for outpatient care.
Directs that the VA-chosen care be provided at the VA facility closest to the member's location.
Document 3: Directs the President to develop a comprehensive interagency strategy for long-term security and stability in Pakistan.
Authorizes appropriations for Pakistan and the Pakistan Counterinsurgency Capability Fund.
Document 4: Directs the Department of Defense to submit a plan to enhance security of military personnel at DOD installations.
Requires implementation of the plan recommendations within 90 days after enactment.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish a board of review for mental health determinations of separated individuals.
Requires notification of rights to separation review for covered individuals.
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to transfer necessary amounts to continue pay for members of the Armed Forces during funding gaps.
Prohibits transfer after December 31, 2011.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Defense to submit a policy for collecting and reporting suicide data involving Armed Forces members.
Includes reserve components and their dependents.
Document 8: Directs the President to develop a comprehensive interagency strategy for long-term security and stability in Afghanistan.
Authorizes appropriations for Afghanistan.
Document 9: Amends the National Security Act to require intelligence sharing regarding threats to Armed Forces members.
Requires the Secretary to report annually to Congress about instances of such information.
Document 10: Revises the definition of 'entitlement land' for payments to local governments.
Includes federal land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Defense.

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Labeling
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments to the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act concerning drug labeling, approval processes, and regulations for various drug categories.

Document 1: requires the label of a drug containing an opioid to prominently state that the drug contains an opioid and addiction to the drug is possible.
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
Document 2: deem a drug to be misbranded unless the labeling of such drug lists the identity of the country of manufacture
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
Document 3: requires the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to extend by six months the exclusivity period for a drug
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
Document 4: allows drugs to be compounded and distributed to practitioners
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
Document 5: requires the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to encourage the health care facilities to report on antibacterial drug use
amends the Public Health Service Act
Document 6: disqualify from being a 'first applicant' an applicant submitting an abbreviated new drug application
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
Document 7: prohibit the manufacturer of a brand name drug from authorizing or commencing the manufacturing of a generic version
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
Document 8: establish a reciprocal marketing approval process
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
Document 9: require an application for the approval of a medical device to include a description of any pediatric subpopulations
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
Document 10: permit the Food and Drug Administration to allow the sponsor of a new drug to rely upon information submitted for an approved medication
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act

topic.name: Mental Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving mental health care, research, and support services for individuals, particularly focusing on youth and community-based initiatives.

Document 1: Mental Health Care Provider Retention Act of 2016 allows continued treatment for individuals transitioning from DOD to VA.
The VA shall reimburse DOD for rendered services that otherwise would have been furnished by the VA.
Document 2: Youth Mental Health Research Act authorizes the establishment of a Youth Mental Health Research Network.
NIMH may award grants for conducting youth mental health research and intervention services.
Document 3: Community-Based Mental Health Infrastructure Improvements Act authorizes grants for construction or modernization of community-based mental health services.
The Act aims to improve facilities for mental health and substance abuse services.
Document 4: Youth Mental Health Research Act focuses on early detection and intervention of severe mental illness in young people.
The Director of NIH can award grants for youth mental health research and services.
Document 5: Combating Autism Reauthorization Act establishes the National Institute of Autism Spectrum Disorders for research and services.
It requires the Secretary of HHS to establish grant programs related to autism spectrum disorders.
Document 9: Children's Recovery from Trauma Act supports programs for treating children and youth with mental disorders from traumatic events.
HHS must oversee the National Child Traumatic Stress Initiative and promote evidence-based practices.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at supporting agricultural practices, disaster relief, and sustainable farming initiatives in the United States.

Document 1: Extends federal procurement of biobased products
Establishes a biochar research and demonstration program
Document 2: Emergency financial assistance to producers on a farm that incurred qualifying natural disaster-caused quantity or quality losses
Provides specified Commodity Credit Corporation funds for grants to state departments of agriculture
Document 3: Limits the pilot project permitting the planting of various crops on base acres
Provides that producers may reduce the base acres for any covered commodity
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to conduct a pilot program
Grants to purchase fresh fruits and vegetables for distribution to schools
Document 5: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008
Requires producers to demonstrate they have entered into a contract to produce a fruit or vegetable crop for processing
Document 6: Includes shellfish as an eligible crop in the noninsured crop disaster assistance program
Document 7: Revises the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing
Document 8: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 regarding covered commodities
Producers on a farm may reduce the base acres for any covered commodity
Document 9: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to consider national average market price variations for different classes of wheat
Document 10: Revises the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing

topic.name: U.S. Sanctions Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions on foreign nations, particularly concerning financial transactions and geopolitical actions.

Document 1: China cannot be allowed to interfere with the free use of waters and airspace in the South China Sea and the East China Sea
the United States should oppose any unilateral Chinese actions to undermine Japan's control of the Senkaku Islands.
Document 2: Congress must act to preempt any move to grant licenses resulting in Iran's access to the U.S. dollar
The President may not issue a license to an offshore dollar-clearing entity to conduct a transaction in U.S. dollars with an Iranian financial institution.
Document 3: prohibits the President from issuing a license that permits a person to conduct an offshore U.S. dollar clearing system for transactions involving the government of Iran
The President shall block and prohibit all transactions in property and property interests of any listed institution.
Document 4: directs the Department of Defense to report to Congress annually on cooperation between Iran and Russia
how cooperation between Iran and Russia has increased with respect to the JCPA.
Document 5: prohibits the President from issuing a license that permits a person to conduct an offshore U.S. dollar clearing system for transactions involving the government of Iran
The President may impose additional sanctions pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.
Document 6: prohibits the Department of the Treasury from issuing a license under any law that would permit a person to conduct offshore dollar clearing outside of the U.S. financial system
for transactions that would involve or benefit the government of Iran or any Iranian person.
Document 7: requires that all U.S.-imposed sanctions with respect to Belarus remain in effect until the President certifies to Congress that the government of Belarus has met specified conditions
the President shall include the Joint Stock Company Belaruskali and the Joint Stock Company Belarusian Potash Company on the list of specially designated nationals.
Document 8: recognizes that there has been a failure of leadership in South Sudan
the President shall transmit to Congress a strategy to guide future U.S. engagement with South Sudan.
Document 9: authorizes the President to provide Ukraine with specified defense articles, services, and training
the President should provide Ukraine with appropriate intelligence and other information.
Document 10: directs the President to conduct a nuclear policy review to consider a range of options on the role of nuclear weapons in U.S. security policy
requires the Secretary of Defense to conduct a comprehensive review of the U.S. nuclear posture.

topic.name: Social Security and Pension Reforms
topic.description: Legislative amendments related to the Social Security Act and pension benefits aimed at enhancing fairness and addressing issues in benefit distribution.

Document 1: Proportionate Final Benefit Act of 2007 - Amends title II of the Social Security Act to provide that an individual's entitlement to any OASDI benefit shall continue through the month of his or her death.
the benefit shall be payable for such month only to the extent proportionate to the number of days in the month preceding the date of death.
Document 2: Social Security Number Fraudulent Use Notification Act of 2007 - Amends title II of the Social Security Act to require the Commissioner of Social Security to notify an individual when the Social Security account number ascribed to the individual does not match relevant Administration records.
the individual's Social Security account number has otherwise been used by any other person in a fraudulent or otherwise illegal manner.
Document 3: Social Security Benefits Fairness Act of 2007 - Amends title II of the Social Security Act to provide that, if an OASDI recipient dies during the first 15 days of a month, the last payment of the monthly benefit for that month shall be half the usual benefit amount.
Document 4: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to provide for increases in widow's and widower's insurance benefits by reason of delayed retirement.
the treatment of delayed retirement in cases of simultaneous entitlement to OASDI benefits and widow's or widower's insurance benefits.
Document 8: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to reduce from 10 to five the number of years of marriage prior to divorce required for a divorced spouse to be eligible for benefits under the OASDI program.

topic.name: Transportation Regulations and Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses regulations and services related to air and rail transportation aimed at improving customer experience, safety, and operational efficiency.

Document 1: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to prescribe regulations to require commercial air carriers to allow each air passenger who has purchased a ticket...
Imposes a civil penalty on air carriers that fail to comply with such regulations.
Document 2: Amends the Aviation and Transportation Security Act to require the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to comply with the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act...
Document 3: Directs the Federal Railroad Administration (FRA) to require each railroad carrier to arrange for an independent inspection by a professional engineer of...
Requires each railroad carrier to provide inspection results to the FRA, and the FRA to make those results available to the public.
Document 4: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to establish an Aviation Rulemaking Committee to develop recommendations for the Administrator...
Requires the Committee to comprise aviation safety experts, labor representatives, and industry stakeholders.
Document 5: Directs the National Railroad Passenger Corporation (Amtrak) to propose a pet policy that allows passengers to transport domesticated cats or dogs on certain Amtrak trains.
Requires Amtrak to collect a fee for each cat or dog transported by a ticketed passenger to cover administrative costs.
Document 6: Directs the city of Los Angeles to transfer the LA/Ontario International Airport to the city of Ontario, California...
Requires the city of Los Angeles to sell such airport to the city of Ontario no later than 60 days after the Administrator's determination.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to issue regulations directing each air carrier to establish a policy to ensure that a family that purchases tickets for a flight is seated together during that flight...
Defines 'family' to mean a grouping of individuals that...includes a child age 12 years or younger.
Document 8: Amends the Safe, Accountable, Flexible, Efficient Transportation Equity Act: A Legacy for Users (SAFETEA-LU) to make eligible for use of a state's apportionment of coordinated border infrastructure program funds...
Reauthorizes the program through FY2015, indexed for inflation.
Document 9: Eliminates certain grounds for waiving Buy American requirements that the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) obligate federal funding for certain air navigation, highway, and rail activities...
Authorizes the Secretary to waive such requirements if the cost of U.S.-produced components and subcomponents is...
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to make capital grants to a class I, class II, or class III railroad or a state or local government for freight rail economic development projects...
Prescribes prevailing wage rate requirements for such projects in accordance with the Davis-Bacon Act.

topic.name: Fisheries Management and Conservation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the management, conservation, and protection of fishery resources in U.S. waters.

Document 1: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to add the state of New York as a member of the New England Fishery Management Council.
Document 2: Amends the High Seas Driftnet Fishing Moratorium Protection Act to direct the Secretary of Commerce to list a nation in the biennial report on international compliance if the nation's fishing vessels are or have been engaged in fishing activities that target or incidentally catch sharks.
Makes it a prohibited act to remove any shark fin at sea.
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery and report to Congress.
Document 8: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to require the Secretary to annually make available funding for Council research and monitoring priorities.

topic.name: Child Labor in Logging Industry
topic.description: Legislation concerning employment of minors in logging operations and associated labor regulations.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to make the restrictions on oppressive child labor inapplicable to employment of an employee age 16 or 17 by a parent, or a person standing in place of a parent, in a logging or mechanized operation owned or operated by that parent or person.
Document 2: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to make the restrictions on oppressive child labor inapplicable to employment of an employee age 16 or 17 by a parent, or a person standing in place of a parent, in a logging or mechanized operation owned or operated by that parent or person.
Document 3: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to make the restrictions on oppressive child labor inapplicable to employment of an employee age 16 or 17 by a parent, or a person standing in place of a parent, in a logging or mechanized operation owned or operated by that parent or person.
Document 10: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to make the restrictions on oppressive child labor inapplicable to employment of an employee age 16 or 17 exclusively by a parent, or a person standing in place of a parent, in a logging operation owned or operated by that parent or person.

topic.name: Telecommunications Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts related to telecommunications, public safety, and digital broadcasting.

Document 1: Amends the Digital Television Transition and Public Safety Act of 2005
require that coupons that can be applied toward the purchase of digital-to-analog converter boxes expire
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934
increase the electromagnetic spectrum allocation for public safety services
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934
require that radios designed to receive both satellite digital radio and terrestrial AM or FM radio broadcasts be capable of receiving and playing digital terrestrial AM or FM broadcasts.
Document 4: Amends the Internet Tax Freedom Act
make permanent the ban on state and local taxation of Internet access
Document 5: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to initiate a notice of inquiry
consider measures to examine the existence and availability of advanced blocking technologies
Document 6: Amends the Communications Act of 1934
require telecommunication carriers to provide, at the request of a law enforcement agency, call location information
Document 7: Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to initiate a proceeding
reevaluate and reconfigure its band (spectrum) plans
Document 8: Amends the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act
require the National Telecommunications and Information Administration (NTIA) to post on its website detailed transition plans
Document 9: Amends the Digital Television Transition and Public Safety Act of 2005
require that coupons that can be applied toward the purchase of digital-to-analog converter boxes expire six months after issuance
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to make competitive grants to court reporting systems
to promote recruitment, training, and placement of individuals

topic.name: Data Privacy and Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection of personally identifiable information and sensitive data across various sectors.

Document 1: Amends provisions of the federal criminal code authorizing a video tape service provider to disclose personally identifiable information concerning any consumer to any person with the informed, written consent of the consumer
require such consent be in a form distinct and separate from any form setting forth other legal or financial obligations of the consumer
Document 2: prohibit researchers from disclosing such information from federally funded research to persons not connected to the research
Disclosures of such information are permitted if required by law, necessary for the medical treatment of the research subject, made with the consent of the subject
Document 3: ensure that protected health information and personally identifiable information are not compromised during transmission
employ access control methodologies to share protected information solely in accordance with state laws and regulations
Document 4: deems the submission of information by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to be a covered disclosure of certain records maintained on individuals
a permitted disclosure under regulations promulgated under specified provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996
Document 5: requires the Government Publishing Office to establish and maintain a website that provides the public free electronic access to all congressionally mandated reports
must redact from such a report any information that may not be publicly released under the Freedom of Information Act
Document 6: requires individuals, corporations, or other non-government entities that access, maintain, communicate, or handle sensitive account information or nonpublic personal information to implement an information security program
to notify consumers, federal law enforcement, appropriate administrative agencies of certain data breaches
Document 7: require federal agencies to make disclosable public records available in an electronic, publicly accessible format
prohibit an agency from withholding information under FOIA unless such agency reasonably foresees that disclosure would cause specific identifiable harm
Document 8: establish an opt-out list of consumers who have notified the CFPB that they do not wish to allow it to collect personally identifiable information about them
Requires the CFPB to notify consumers publicly of such a breach on the front page of its website
Document 9: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable protected health information
liable to any person whose personally identifiable covered information has been disclosed
Document 10: provide individuals who have been affected by a breach of personnel records with complimentary identity protection coverage
establish a task force to develop specific recommendations on mitigating adverse consequences relating to data breaches

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents pertains to various legislative measures aimed at the conservation and management of wildlife species and their habitats in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to allow an agricultural producer... to take migratory game birds by the aid of baiting
a local representative of the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) confirms that the crop has been destroyed
Document 2: Grants the Secretary of the Interior permanent authority to authorize any state to issue electronic duck stamps
Sets forth state electronic duck stamp application requirements.
Document 3: Amends the Lacey Act Amendments of 1981 to prohibit any person from importing, exporting, transporting, selling... any prohibited wildlife species
Defines 'breeding' as facilitating the propagation or reproduction... of a prohibited wildlife species.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to establish Southern Sea Otter Military Readiness Areas for national defense purposes
Allows certain incidental otter takings in such Areas in the course of a military readiness activity.
Document 5: Amends the Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp Act to allow the Department of the Interior... to set the cost for Federal Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamps
Limits the cost of the stamp to $25 for the first five years and $30 after that.
Document 6: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to prohibit any regulation that goes in effect after February 24, 2014, from prohibiting or restricting the possession, sale... of elephant ivory that has been lawfully imported
changing any methods of, or standards for, determining if ivory has been lawfully imported.
Document 7: Prohibits the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) or the Secretary of the Interior from implementing or limiting any modification to a public or private land-related policy... relating to the listing of the lesser prairie chicken as a threatened species
Document 8: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to require the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Commerce... to provide to affected states all data that is used as the basis of a determination on whether a species is an endangered species
Requires the appropriate Secretary to use data submitted by a state, tribal, or county government in making such a determination.
Document 9: This bill amends the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act to make it one of the purposes of the Act to extend financial and technical assistance to the states for the promotion of hunting and recreational shooting
Amounts apportioned to the states... may be used for hunter recruitment and recreational shooter recruitment.
Document 10: Authorizes the Secretary of a military department... to make payments to a conservation banking program or 'in-lieu-fee' mitigation sponsor approved in accordance with the Federal Guidance for the Establishment, Use and Operation of Mitigation Banks
Allows such payments... for military construction to be treated as eligible costs of the construction project.

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative efforts aimed at supporting and enhancing small businesses in the United States through funding, technical assistance, and regulatory reforms.

Document 1: Investing in America's Small Businesses Act of 2016
require the Community Development Financial Institutions Fund to make grants to community development financial institutions
Document 2: Strengthening Technical Assistance, Resources, and Training to Unleash the Potential of Veterans Act of 2016
require the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make two-year renewable grants to create and operate incubators and accelerators
Document 3: Small Business Investment Company Capital Act of 2015
increase from $225 million to $350 million the maximum amount of outstanding leverage
Document 4: Small Business Regulatory Sunset Act of 2015
requires each federal agency to establish a plan for the periodic review of its rules that have a significant economic impact on a substantial number of small entities
Document 5: Small Business Innovation Impact Act
requires the Chief Counsel for Advocacy of the Small Business Administration to submit an analysis on the investment of small businesses in developing new and useful inventions
Document 6: Commercial Real Estate and Economic Development Act of 2015
reinstates the authority for Small Business Administration (SBA) low-interest refinancing of small business debt
Document 7: SBIR and STTR Reauthorization and Improvement Act of 2016
reauthorizes and makes permanent the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
Document 8: amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958
require the Small Business Administration (SBA) to give priority to small business investment company (SBIC) applications for licenses to operate in a declared major disaster area
Document 9: Education for Contracting Personnel Improvement Act of 2016
require the Small Business Administration (SBA) at least annually to provide a list of all changes made in the prior year to regulations
Document 10: America Realizing the Informational Skills and Initiative of New Graduates Act of 2010
establish a program of grants to eligible small business employers to defray the cost of compensation they pay to recent graduates

topic.name: Land Management and Designation
topic.description: This cluster of documents primarily discusses various legislative acts related to land management, designation of trails, and property exchanges involving federal lands.

Document 1: applies whenever the Department of Agriculture (USDA), through the Forest Service, prepares an environmental assessment
the bill repeals the Merchantable Timber Contracting Pilot Program
Document 2: establish as part of the national trails system national discovery trails
requires the appropriate Department for each national discovery trail to administer the trail in cooperation with a competent trailwide volunteer-based organization
Document 3: directs the Department of Agriculture to sell and quitclaim all interest of the United States in and to a parcel of real property
Document 7: authorizes the Department of the Interior, through the National Park Service, to convey to the Veterans of Foreign Wars Post 5699 approximately 1.542 acres of real property
land and interests acquired by the United States by this bill shall be administered as part of the Gulf Islands National Seashore
Document 9: designates as the Alex Diekmann Peak the 9,765-foot peak located 2.2 miles west-northwest of Finger Mountain

topic.name: Election Reform Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative efforts aimed at reforming election processes and campaign financing in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals
to domestic corporations in which one or more foreign principals directly or indirectly has an ownership interest.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to limit expenditures by candidates for the House of Representatives
Sets at $250,000 the maximum amount of expenditures which may be made for a runoff election.
Document 3: Requires in each election for federal office for inclusion on the ballot after the names of all candidates a separate line for 'None of the Above.'
Requires a new election within 30 days if 'None of the Above' receives the most votes.
Document 4: Directs the Election Assistance Commission to reimburse each eligible state for the costs incurred in establishing an online voter registration program
meeting specified requirements.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit foreign nationals from making contributions or donations
in connection with state and local ballot initiatives and referenda.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to limit expenditures by candidates for the House of Representatives
Sets at $250,000 the maximum amount of expenditures which may be made for a runoff election.
Document 7: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to prohibit a state or local election official from accepting a challenge to an individual's eligibility to register to vote
if the challenge is not based on good cause.
Document 8: This bill amends the Revised Statutes to establish the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November
as the days for the election, in each state and territory, of Delegates to, or Members of, Congress.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require any person conducting a federal election phone bank to report to the Federal Election Commission specified information
Limits such requirement to projects contacting at least 1,500 households during the 25 days before a federal election.
Document 10: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nations
to domestic corporations with respect to which one or more foreign principals serves on the board of directors.

topic.name: U.S. Trade Policy and Enforcement
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures aimed at addressing trade practices, enforcing trade laws, and protecting U.S. economic interests in international trade.

Document 1: China Hurts Economic Advancement Through Subsidies Act or CHEATS Act
authorize the administering authority or the U.S. International Trade Commission (USITC) to impose countervailing duties
Document 2: Directs the U.S. Customs and Border Protection to provide for the reliquidation
refund of collected excess antidumping duties
Document 3: Trade Enforcement Priorities Act
revise requirements for identification of trade enforcement priorities by the United States Trade Representative (USTR)
Document 4: Border Tax Fairness Act
pursue World Trade Organization (WTO) negotiations that result in revisions to WTO agreements
Document 5: Currency Reform for Fair Trade Act of 2009
require the administering authority to determine whether the exchange rate of the currency of an exporting country is fundamentally misaligned
Document 6: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to make eligible for the drawback
refund of duties paid on certain imported merchandise
Document 7: Nonmarket Economy Trade Remedy Act of 2009
apply countervailing duties to nonmarket economies
Document 9: U.S. Trade Representative (USTR) to report to Congress annually
impose duties on merchandise from China in an amount equivalent to the estimated annual loss of revenue

topic.name: U.S. Territorial and Indigenous Affairs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies aimed at addressing the needs and rights of U.S. territories, insular areas, and indigenous populations.

Document 1: Establish within the Empowering Insular Communities activity a team of technical, policy, and financial experts to develop an energy action plan addressing the energy needs of each of the insular areas.
Assist each of the insular areas and Freely Associated States in implementing the plan.
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of Labor from issuing any special wage certificate under the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to certain entities, allowing them to pay individuals with disabilities subminimum wages.
Document 3: Reauthorize appropriations for the Administration on Aging, including the Eldercare Locator Service and pension counseling and information programs.
Grants for Native Americans.
Document 5: Amends the Native American Programs Act of 1974 to reauthorize appropriations for programs that promote the economic and social self-sufficiency of Native Americans.
Assist Native Americans in ensuring the survival and continuing vitality of their languages.
Document 6: Establishes the U.S. Office of Hawaiian Relations within the Office of the Secretary of the Interior.
Recognizes the right of the qualified Native Hawaiian constituents to reorganize the single Native Hawaiian governing entity.
Document 9: Amends the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 to extend housing assistance for Native Americans and loan guarantees for Native Hawaiian housing.

topic.name: Abortion and Reproductive Rights
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislation related to abortion, reproductive health, and associated rights and protections.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of State from contributing to any organization or program that supports or participates in the management of a program of coercive abortion or involuntary sterilization.

Defines 'supports or participates in' as providing material, financial, or technical support to any government, agency, or nongovernmental organization that enforces or implements a program that includes coercive abortion or involuntary sterilization.
Document 2: Prohibits the federal government and any state or local government receiving federal financial assistance from subjecting an institutional or individual health care entity to discrimination on the basis that the entity refuses to participate in abortion-related activities.
Creates a cause of action for any violation of the abortion discrimination prohibition.
Document 10: Prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control or population planning program or family planning activity (including any abortion procedure).

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the U.S. Postal Service and its operations, including postal benefits for military personnel and the issuance of commemorative stamps.

1: Expands the enumerated powers of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to include the authority to provide nonpostal services.
Removes existing conditions that limit nonpostal services to certain services offered as of January 1, 2006.
2: Directs the Secretary of Defense to provide for a program under which postal benefits are provided to a member of the Armed Forces who is on active duty.
Provides the postal benefits in the form of coupons or other evidence of credit (vouchers) to use for postal-free mailings.
3: Directs the Postmaster General to provide for the issuance of a forever stamp to honor the sacrifices of families who have lost a loved one who was a member of the Armed Forces in combat.
4: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to make the Postmaster General or Postmaster General designee the importer of record for non-letter class mail imported into the United States.
Imposes a duty of $1 on each item of non-letter class mail imported into the United States.
5: Makes procedures governing the closure or consolidation of postal branches, stations, or other facilities the same as those applicable to post offices.
6: Directs the Postmaster General to provide for the issuance of a forever stamp that honors the sacrifices of the members of the Armed Forces who have been prisoners, missing, or unaccounted for.
Depicts the National League of Families POW/MIA flag.
7: Deems any payment required to be made on or before a prescribed date that is delivered by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) after such date to be received by the payee on the date of the U.S. postmark stamped on the envelope.
Applies the provisions of this Act only if the postmark date falls on or before the prescribed date for making the payment.
8: Removes the authority of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to issue semipostal stamps except as provided by an Act of Congress.
Makes entities eligible to receive amounts that become available from the sale of semipostals.
9: Repeals the service standards for market-dominant products implemented by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) on July 1, 2012.
Directs USPS to reinstate the service standards that were in effect on December 31, 2011.
10: Directs the Secretary of Defense to provide for a program under which postal benefits are provided to a member of the Armed Forces who is on active duty.
Provides the postal benefits in the form of coupons or other evidence of credit (vouchers) to use for postal-free mailings.

topic.name: Environmental Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments related to environmental protection, mine reclamation, and worker health compensation.

Document 1: Amends the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act
advises on the review and approval of the site exposure matrix
Document 2: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977
permit the use of moneys in the Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund
Document 3: North Fork Watershed Protection Act of 2010
Withdraws federally owned lands from mining laws
Document 4: extends a state's shield from federal liability
implementing a state abandoned mine reclamation plan
Document 5: Chetco River Protection Act
withdraws federal land from mining laws
Document 6: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act
authorize uncertified states and Indian tribes to use certain unexpended funds
Document 7: prohibit payments from the Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund
to a state or Indian tribe certified as having completed coal reclamation
Document 8: repeal the $15 million limitation
total annual payment to certified states or Indian tribes
Document 9: EEOICPA Ombudsman Extension and Enhancement Act
assist in making claims for benefits
Document 10: Mohave County Radiation Compensation Act
include the county of Mohave among affected areas for compensation

topic.name: Commemorative Legislation and Memorials
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts that establish commemorative events, memorials, and recognition of historical figures and events in the United States.

Document 1: Recognizing the seven decades of Christian church services being held in the Capitol
Requires the AOC to display it permanently in a place of prominence
Document 2: Mint and issue up to 100,000 $10 coins emblematic of the warrior ethos of the U.S. Marine Corps
Surcharges received to be paid promptly to the Marine Corps Heritage Foundation
Document 3: Authorize the Secretary of Defense to convey the Mt. Soledad Veterans Memorial
Located within the Soledad Natural Park in San Diego, California
Document 4: Promote recreational opportunities for youth and commemorate women's history
Relinquishes its reversionary interest in specified Tulare Railroad Proposed Parcels
Document 5: Allows the family of a deceased law enforcement officer to request a U.S. flag
Supplied by the Architect of the Capitol
Document 8: Establishes the Ronald Reagan Centennial Commission
Plan and carry out activities to honor Ronald Reagan on the occasion of the 100th anniversary of his birth
Document 10: Requires flags of the United States acquired for use by the federal government to be entirely manufactured in the United States

topic.name: Regulatory Compliance and Enforcement
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts that enhance regulatory compliance and enforcement mechanisms across various sectors including civil rights, financial transactions, and pipeline safety.

Document 1: Amends the Inspector General Act of 1978 to authorize each federal agency inspector general...
comparison of records shall not be considered a matching program for purposes of the Privacy Act of 1974.
Document 2: Limits criminal penalties for violations of reporting requirements imposed on financial institutions...
Limits the amount of property forfeiture and monetary penalties imposed with respect to a monetary instrument transaction.
Document 3: Directs the Attorney General to designate a Deputy Chief in the Criminal Section of the Civil Rights Division...
authorizes the Attorney General to award grants to state or local law enforcement agencies for the investigation and prosecution of such cases.
Document 4: amends the Natural Gas Act to require that civil penalties for violations of the Act be assessed...
guidelines which it apply regarding civil penalties for violations under the Federal Power Act.
Document 5: Requires each operator of a gas pipeline facility...to accelerate the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of gas piping or equipment that is leaking...
Directs the Administrator of the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration to issue non-binding best practices guidelines.
Document 6: directs the Department of Transportation (DOT) to require the person purchasing or operating a gas or hazardous liquid pipeline facility to inspect it...
The inspection requirement shall apply with respect to newly listed abandoned pipelines.
Document 9: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to require the operator of a drilling and production operation...
Subjects the chief executive officer to a civil penalty for non-compliance with this requirement.
Document 10: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit...embezzling, stealing, obtaining by fraud or deception...
Prescribes criminal and civil penalties for violations, including a civil penalty of up to the greater of 3 times the economic loss attributable to the violation or $1 million.

topic.name: Social Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving social welfare programs and addressing issues related to public assistance, unemployment, and healthcare for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 to extend the benefit calculation of 113.6 % of the June 2008 value of the thrifty food plan for the supplemental nutrition assistance program (SNAP)
extends the benefit calculation for the supplemental nutrition assistance program (SNAP, formerly known as food stamps)
Document 2: Amends title III (Grants to States for Unemployment Compensation) of the Social Security Act to require state laws to deny unemployment compensation to applicants unless they undergo drug testing
Deny unemployment compensation for five years to any applicant who tests positive for drugs three or more times
Document 4: Prohibits terminating the eligibility of an individual who qualifies for treatment as a dependent under TRICARE solely by reason of exceeding the age limit
Requires the continuation of such coverage for military families
Document 5: Amends the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996 to repeal the denial to drug felons of eligibility for benefits under the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) program
Document 6: Requires a state that is receiving funds for certain law enforcement assistance programs to adopt laws and policies that prohibit parole for individuals convicted of a criminal sexual offense against a victim who is elderly
Grants states three years to implement such laws and policies

topic.name: Federal Legislative Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various federal legislative acts aimed at amending existing laws related to judicial procedures, health and safety regulations, and accountability in government operations.

Document 1: Amends federal bankruptcy, criminal, and civil law, as well as the Classified Information Procedures Act and the Controlled Substances Act
to harmonize them with recent amendments to the federal time-computation rules
Document 2: Grants U.S. district courts original jurisdiction over any civil action for damages for personal injury or death
Makes this Act applicable to any action pending on or commenced after January 1, 2002.
Document 3: Amends the federal judicial code to allow U.S. courts to hear cases against a foreign state's corporate affiliates
is attributable to any corporate affiliate of the agency or instrumentality that: (1) directly or indirectly owns a majority of shares
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to identify and establish standards for contaminants in bottled water
Requires the Secretary to: (1) promulgate regulations that require each manufacturer of bottled water to submit reports on its water
Document 5: Amends the Egg Products Inspection Act to revise housing and treatment of egg-laying hens
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to delegate to the California Department of Food and Agriculture certain enforcement authority
Document 6: Sets forth additional factors for the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to consider in determining the payment amount for new clinical diagnostic laboratory tests
Requires the Secretary to justify publicly the basis and rate determination for any payments made under the clinical laboratory fee schedule
Document 7: Authorizes a six-month open enrollment period starting 30 days after enactment of this Act for a federal judicial official to opt into the Judicial Survivors' Annuities System
Allows judicial officials enrolled in the System before or after enactment of this Act to purchase, in three-month increments, up to an additional year of service credit
Document 8: Amends the federal judicial code to provide for an additional judgeship for the western district of Michigan
increasing the number of judgeships from four to five.
Document 9: Authorizes the Comptroller General to conduct reviews, including onsite examinations, of any credit facility established by the Federal Reserve Board
Specifies the credit facilities to which this Act applies.

topic.name: Veterans and Refugee Assistance Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative efforts aimed at providing support and benefits to veterans, military families, and specific refugee populations.

Document 1: extends special survivor indemnity allowance for surviving spouses of deceased Armed Forces members
Document 2: deems Cadet Nurse Corps service as active military for VA purposes
Document 3: makes immigrant visas available to individuals born in Tibet
Document 4: makes immigrant visas available to individuals born in Tibet
Document 5: directs citizenship for individuals who died in the Binghamton shooting
Document 8: establishes a Commission on Wartime Relocation and Internment of Latin Americans of Japanese descent
Document 9: appropriates funds for survivor benefits for deceased servicemembers
Document 10: establishes a National Veterans Support Network to assist military families and veterans

topic.name: Public Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving public health, safety standards, and energy policies in the United States.

Document 1: establish a program to award competitive grants to state and local governments for the design, purchase, and installation of qualifying solar equipment
consider financial need and best practices to ensure maximum efficiency
Document 2: reauthorize appropriations for the Select Agent Program
requires appropriate training of individuals handling or using such agents and toxins
Document 3: prohibit siting, constructing, expanding, or operating a liquefied natural gas import terminal
sets forth terms and conditions for application approval
Document 4: prescribe minimum safety standards to require gas or hazardous liquid pipelines
requires DOT inspection and testing of intrastate gas pipelines
Document 5: establish the Epidemiology and Laboratory Capacity Grant Program
improving surveillance for, and response to, infectious diseases
Document 6: assist public health agencies in improving surveillance
address documented workforce shortages in state and local health departments
Document 7: evaluate best practices in public health surveillance
develop improved standards and protocols for binational epidemiology
Document 8: establish an advisory council on prostate cancer
carry out a national campaign to increase awareness
Document 9: prohibit unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the provision of funeral goods or services
require cemeteries to provide consumers all written rules and regulations
Document 10: establish a program to make grants to accredited schools of allopathic or osteopathic medicine
increase the number of recent allopathic and osteopathic medical school graduates who practice in underserved rural communities

topic.name: Federal Research and Policy in Science
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to federal initiatives aimed at improving scientific research, public access to scientific information, and preparedness for natural disasters and climate change.

Document 1: Establishes a national forensic science research program to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences.
Directs NSF to create a merit-reviewed, competitive forensic science research grants program.
Document 2: Directs each of the covered agencies to formulate and implement a public access policy to make covered works publicly available.
Specifies that the public access policy shall allow the public to read, download, and analyze by machine covered works in digital form.
Document 6: Requires the National Science Foundation to establish a Global Warming Education Program to broaden the understanding of human induced global warming.
Emphasizes actionable information to help people understand and to promote implementation of new technologies.
Document 8: Directs the Administrator of FEMA to establish a program to make grants to state and local governments to assist them in projects that improve the ability of trauma center hospitals and/or airports in hazardous seismic zones to withstand an earthquake.

topic.name: Government Oversight and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at enhancing government oversight, accountability, and reform in various sectors, including finance, housing, and regulatory practices.

Document 1: authorizes the Special Inspector General to exercise specified employment authorities for additional personnel
conduct, supervise, and coordinate an audit or investigation of any action taken under such Act
Document 2: directs the Comptroller of the Currency to provide for the establishment and chartering of Federal Financial Services and Credit Companies
subjects FFSCCs to specified requirements
Document 3: eliminates the mandatory printing of bills and resolutions
requires the Public Printer to make bills and resolutions available only in an electronic format
Document 4: revises requirements for the apportionment of state planning funds to metropolitan planning organizations
prioritizes the needs of high performing MPOs
Document 5: reauthorizes and revises requirements for assistance with respect to housing for low- and moderate-income families
requires mortgagees to engage in reasonable loss mitigation activities
Document 6: directs the Corporation for National and Community Service to award competitive, matching, three-year grants
requires partnership grantees to use at least 80% of their grant for competitive subgrants
Document 7: requires each federal agency to publish in the Federal Register a plan for the periodic review of its major rules
requires the review to include a cost-benefit analysis of the rule
Document 8: establishes the Bipartisan Earmark Reform Commission
requires the Majority Leaders of the House of Representatives and of the Senate to introduce legislation recommended in the report
Document 9: establishes the Senate Committee to Reduce Government Waste
recommend to the Senate actions designed to modify or eliminate underperforming programs
Document 10: requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to ensure that advance notice of a covert test is not provided to any individual
directs the Secretary to study the impact of implementing covert testing procedures on transportation security

topic.name: Terrorism Legislation and Preparedness
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislation related to terrorism prevention, response, and insurance.

Document 1: Makes permanent the provisions of the USA PATRIOT Act
impose criminal penalties for providing material support or resources to a perpetrator of international terrorism
Document 2: establish a Community Preparedness Division in the Department of Homeland Security
develop and implement educational programs for the public on terrorism and natural disaster preparedness
Document 6: Amends the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002 to extend the Terrorism Risk Insurance program
requires reports on the findings of the President's Working Group on Financial Markets
Document 8: Amends the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002 to extend the Terrorism Insurance Program until December 31, 2019
requires the Secretary of the Treasury to collect all required terrorism loss risk-spreading premiums

topic.name: Health and HIV/AIDS Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing health issues, particularly related to HIV/AIDS and the healthcare needs of specific populations.

Document 1: Amends the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 to make Antigua and Barbuda, the Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Dominica, Grenada, Jamaica, Montserrat, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Saint Lucia, Suriname, Trinidad and Tobago, and the Dominican Republic eligible for assistance to combat HIV/AIDS.
Document 2: Amends the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 to provide assistance to combat HIV/AIDS in India.
Document 4: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to develop and implement an ongoing and sustainable national strategy for identifying and evaluating the health status and health care needs of Native Hawaiians and Other Pacific Islanders.
Document 9: Directs the President to establish an integrated, five-year Global HIV/AIDS-Free Generation Strategy to expand efforts to combat global HIV/AIDS.

topic.name: Homeownership Assistance Programs
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at promoting and preserving homeownership through various financial assistance and modification programs.

Document 1: Preserving American Homeownership Act of 2013
shared appreciation mortgage modifications
Document 2: Rural Health Care Capital Access Reauthorization Act of 2011
exemption for critical access hospitals
Document 3: Preserving American Homeownership Act of 2012
pilot program to encourage shared appreciation mortgage modifications
Document 4: Homeownership Preservation Education Act of 2012
evaluate effectiveness of reducing defaults on residential mortgages
Document 5: Second Chance at Homeownership Act of 2012
create a voluntary FHA program to provide lenders for qualified second-chance borrowers
Document 9: Protecting Habitat Homeownership Act
exempt residential mortgage loans originated by non-profit low-income housing providers from certain minimum loan standards

topic.name: Government Accountability and Fairness
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures aimed at ensuring accountability, fairness, and transparency in government operations and services.

Document 1: Fairness in Flood Mapping Act of 2010 requires reimbursement for flood mapping services.
Ensures fairness for property owners in flood mapping.
Document 3: Transparency and Accountability in State and Local Contracting Act amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act.
Requires federal agencies to adjust limits on administrative expenditures for oversight.
Document 5: Military Separation Pay Fairness Act directs DOD to ensure fair separation pay for former members of the Armed Forces.
Addresses historical injustices related to separation pay due to homosexuality.
Document 6: Court-Appointed Guardian Accountability and Senior Protection Act aims to assess and improve adult guardianship proceedings.
Focuses on accountability in the appointment and monitoring of guardians.
Document 9: Establishes the Commission on Congressional Budgetary Accountability and Review of Federal Agencies.
Requires systematic assessment of agency program effectiveness.

topic.name: Housing Finance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to government-sponsored enterprises (GSEs) and housing finance reforms.

Document 1: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to carry out a one-year program providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages.
Defines a qualified mortgage as one... that is owned or guaranteed by the particular GSE.
Document 2: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to carry out a one-year program providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages.
Specifies the terms and conditions of a refinancing mortgage, including a 40-year term to maturity and a prohibition on borrower fees.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Housing Enterprises Financial Safety and Soundness Act of 1992 to authorize the Federal Housing Finance Agency to revoke the charters of such enterprises.
Requires the FHFA to revoke the particular GSE's charter not later than the winding up or termination of the limited-life regulated entity established with respect to such GSE.

topic.name: Legal Protections and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legal acts aimed at establishing protections and regulations related to witness security, environmental standards, and criminal justice procedures.

Document 1: Amends the Solid Waste Disposal Act to provide that no person shall be liable under any federal, state, or local law...
Document 2: directs the Department of Justice (DOJ) to award competitive matching grants to state, local, and tribal governments to establish or maintain witness protection programs...
Document 3: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to state that nothing in the FISA definition of 'electronic surveillance' shall be construed to encompass surveillance directed at a person located outside the United States.
Document 4: Revises provisions concerning pre-trial investigations of charges and specifications under the Uniform Code of Military Justice (UCMJ)...
Document 5: Directs the Attorney General to make competitive grants to state, tribal, and local governments to establish or maintain programs that provide protection or assistance to witnesses...
Document 6: Directs the Attorney General to make competitive grants to state, tribal, and local governments to establish or maintain programs to protect or assist witnesses...
Document 7: Amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to direct the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF) to make a video recording of the entire process of its examination and testing of an item for the purpose of determining whether the item is a firearm...
Document 8: Amends the federal criminal code to render inadmissible as evidence in the prosecution of a federal felony all prior and subsequent statements by an individual during a custodial interrogation that are not electronically recorded...
Document 9: Repeals a provision of the federal criminal code requiring a provider of electronic communication service or remote computing service to disclose to a governmental entity local and long distance telephone connection records...
Document 10: This bill amends the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 to specify that an unlawful employment practice is established when the complaining party demonstrates that age or participation in investigations...

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
