topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislation aimed at promoting renewable energy sources and improving energy efficiency.

1: availability of 200% of the volume of renewable fuels required
reduction of carbon dioxide emissions from the production and use of renewable fuels
2: Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy Fund
energy programs to be funded with such fees, including wind and solar energy research and development
3: installation of wind turbines and other renewable energy generating technology
4: authorize the President to waive renewable fuels volume requirement
if the fuel concerned is not commercially available
5: provide grants to local educational agencies for clean energy projects
implementing clean energy projects that include measures to conserve energy
6: expand the availability of alternative fuels to consumers
funds to construction or expansion of alternative fueling infrastructure

topic.name: Veterans Affairs and Support Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at providing educational assistance, support services, and resources for veterans and their families.

Document 1: ensures that a veteran may receive Post-9/11 educational assistance payments through direct deposit.
Document 4: make available on the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) website redacted records and documents created by the VA as part of a medical quality-assurance program.
Document 5: Authorizes monthly annuity payments under the Survivor Benefit Plan (SBP) to a supplemental or special needs trust established for the sole benefit of a disabled dependent child.
Document 6: establish the Wounded Warrior K-9 Corps program for the awarding of grants to nonprofits to assist them in establishing programs to provide assistance dogs to certain members of the Armed Forces and veterans.

topic.name: Maritime Legislation and Environmental Policy
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. maritime laws and regulations aimed at environmental protection, oil spill liability, and harbor maintenance.

Document 1: Amends the Security and Accountability for Every Port Act of 2006 to require a notice of arrival (NOA) on the Outer Continental Shelf.
requires a notice of arrival (NOA) from certain U.S. documented vessels.
Document 2: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to make the party responsible for an offshore facility liable for all discharge removal costs and damages.
Makes this Act effective on April 15, 2010.
Document 3: Requires the total budget resources for expenditures from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund for harbor maintenance programs to equal the level of receipts plus interest.
Limits the use of such resources to such programs only.
Document 4: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to remove the limit on liability of a party responsible for an offshore facility.
Makes this Act effective on April 15, 2010.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to award competitive grants to seaport governing bodies for the acquisition of fuel efficient and low-emission equipment.
Sets aside up to 10% of federal funding for research and development of fuel efficient port vehicle or vessel technologies.
Document 6: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to remove the limit on liability of a party responsible for an offshore facility.
from which oil is discharged into or upon navigable waters or adjoining shorelines.
Document 7: Amends the Coast Guard and Maritime Transportation Act of 2006 to expand the directive to promote the transportation of imports of liquefied natural gas.
to include the promotion of the transportation of exports of the gas from the United States.
Document 8: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit the importation or shipment of the species of mussels of the genus Dreissena.
thus covering quagga mussels.
Document 9: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to waive the requirement that Canadian vessels report immediately to the nearest customs facility upon arrival.
if, in fact, they do not anchor or dock at any harbor within the U.S. customs territory.
Document 10: Adjusts the boundary of the Apostle Islands National Lakeshore in Wisconsin to include the Ashland Harbor Breakwater Light Addition.
Authorizes the Secretary to operate, maintain, keep, locate, inspect, repair, and replace any federal aid to navigation.

topic.name: Federal Budget Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving transparency, accountability, and efficiency in the federal budgeting process and agency operations.

Document 1: require the Commissioner of Social Security to submit the annual budget of the Social Security Administration directly to Congress
prohibit the Commissioner from closing or otherwise limiting public access to an SSA field office
Document 2: Requires the President to submit with materials related to each federal budget transmitted to Congress
each proposed expenditure to be justified as if it were a new expenditure
Document 3: requires the inspectors general of federal agencies to supervise, direct, and control audit and investigative activities
Document 4: revise the deadline for comments by the Congressional Budget Office (CBO) and the Government Accountability Office (GAO) on information contained in specified quarterly reports
Document 8: Requires the head of each federal agency to prepare for a fiscal year and submit to the Comptroller General three quarterly and one annual consolidated financial statement
the President's annual budget to include a summary of how the use of accrual accounting procedures would affect the estimated expenditures
Document 9: Directs the Comptroller General to include in the annual report to Congress a list of the most common grounds for sustaining protests relating to bids for contracts
